hypothesis.mechanism: A single contiguous N-byte FOURCC equality gate (`chunk_name == png_sPLT`) admits no incremental coverage edges, so blind search must hit the full constant by chance (~1/256^N). I2S (cmplog) intercepts the wide CMP, records the constant operand 0x73504c54 in the CmpLogObserver, and I2SRandReplace substitutes that literal directly into the input at the matching offset — clearing the gate in one mutation regardless of N. The advantage grows with N because naive's blind-hit probability collapses while I2S substitution stays constant.
hypothesis.program_feature_parameter: GATE_BYTES (compile-time -D) — width in bytes of the magic equality constant; the single difficulty axis (gap ~1/256^GATE_BYTES).
hypothesis.predicted_direction: has_axis fuzzers (cmplog) outperform lacks_axis (naive); ratio grows with GATE_BYTES

predicted_endpoints.GATE_BYTES=1: naive, cmplog, and value_profile all crash readily (~1/256 per exec) — no axis advantage; curves coincide.
predicted_endpoints.GATE_BYTES=8: naive=0 (blind 1/256^8 is unreachable in 600s); cmplog crashes immediately via direct operand substitution; value_profile crashes via Hamming-gradient ascent up the 8-byte compare.

expected_curve_per_fuzzer.naive: monotone-decreasing in GATE_BYTES (crash count collapses ~1/256 per added byte; 0 by GATE_BYTES=4-8)
expected_curve_per_fuzzer.cmplog: flat-high (resolves at every dose; direct I2S substitution is width-insensitive)
expected_curve_per_fuzzer.value_profile: flat-high / mildly slowing (resolves at every dose via byte-by-byte CMP_MAP gradient; per-trial time grows modestly with width but stays bounded)

hypothesis.tradeoff_observation: This is an 'independent' cluster: the same contiguous 4-byte FOURCC equality is cleared by TWO distinct techniques on separate mechanisms — I2S (cmplog) by direct operand substitution, and value_profile by Hamming-gradient ascent — each beating naive on its own. The template names the I2S axis as primary (cmplog vs naive is the highest-resolution decisive pair: 10/10 vs 0/10), but value_profile is carried as an involved fuzzer expected to ALSO beat naive on the same gate, so it is NOT placed in has_axis for the I2S partition; its win is the second, independent decisive pair (value_profile > naive [value_profile]). The cluster's defining property is precisely that both win independently.

verification.dockerfile: one per scan value, see deployed_targets
notes: Member: libpng_7254 sPLT (prompts/BRR-/00_libpng_7254.analysis.json). Source line: `else if (chunk_name == png_sPLT)`.

Two independent decisive pairs at the source branch: cmplog > naive [I2S] (10/10 vs 0/10) and value_profile > naive [value_profile] (8/10 vs 0/10). Both winners clear the SAME 4-byte FOURCC gate via different mechanisms — the 'independent' cluster identity.

Honesty caveat: the schema's has_axis/lacks_axis partition is single-axis (I2S), placing value_profile in lacks_axis for that axis. That is technically correct for the I2S partition but UNDERSTATES the cluster: value_profile is expected to BEAT naive too (its own decisive pair). The verdict adjudicator should evaluate BOTH cmplog>naive AND value_profile>naive, not treat value_profile as a predicted loser.
Singleton cluster (n=1); the dual-technique convergence is inferred from one branch's two decisive pairs, not corroborated across branches.
